---
title: "566 Project Data Exploration"
author: "North Carpenter"
date: "2026-05-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Set working directory for knitting to file location
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path))

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Importing Data

```{r}
# This block will import key data. It is imparative that you structure your
# files in the same way or this will not work. Set up this file in the 
# Y-crash-project folder somewhere on your computer and at the same level of the 
# project folder put the 566 project data folder.
#
# You should be able to import the files in 566 in this chunk only without issue.

setwd("../566 Project Data") # Do not touch

# Import any csv's here
accident2023 <- read.csv("FARS2023NationalCSV/accident.csv")
person2023 <- read.csv("FARS2023NationalCSV/PERSON.csv")
drugs2023 <- read.csv("FARS2023NationalCSV/drugs.csv")
```

```{r}
head(accident2023)
```

```{r}
head(person2023)
```

# Data Overiview

```{r}
looknon <- function(df)
{
  # Take a DF and returns a new df that counts na/"" in one column and 
  # "None"/"none" in another column. Also returns the number of unique values 
  # per column.
  
  df <- df |>
    mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) %>% #make everything char for ==
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "column", values_to = "value") %>%
    group_by(column) %>% # set up groups
    summarise(
      num_missing = sum(is.na(value) | value == "", na.rm = TRUE),
      num_None = sum(value == "None" | value == "none", na.rm = TRUE),
      num_unique  = n_distinct(value)
    ) #summarize to these col
  return(df)
}
```

```{r}
looknon(accident2023)
```

## Pedestrians and Injuries
```{r}
person2023 |>
  group_by(PER_TYPNAME, INJ_SEVNAME) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = INJ_SEVNAME,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  )
```

## Alcohol
```{r}
person2023 |>
  count(ALC_RESNAME)

person2023 |>
  count(DRINKINGNAME)

person2023 |>
  group_by(PER_TYPNAME, DRINKINGNAME) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = DRINKINGNAME,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  )
```

## Drugs
```{r}
drugtype <- drugs2023 |> 
  group_by(DRUGRESNAME) |> 
  summarise(n = n()) |> 
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n))
drugtype

drugs2023 |>
  count(DRUGRESNAME)
```

# Pretty Pictures
```{r}
accident2023noLat <- accident2023 |>
  filter(STATENAME != "Alaska",
         LATITUDE > 24, LATITUDE < 72,
         LONGITUD > -180, LONGITUD < -65)


my_sf <- st_as_sf(accident2023noLat, coords = c('LONGITUD', 'LATITUDE'))

my_sf <- st_set_crs(my_sf, 4326)

#Plot it:

ggplot(my_sf) + 
  geom_sf(aes(color = STATENAME), size = 0.01) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

```{r}
accident2023seattle <- accident2023 |>
  filter(STATENAME == "Connecticut",
         LATITUDE > 24, LATITUDE < 72,
         LONGITUD > -180, LONGITUD < -65)

axSeattleMap <- st_as_sf(accident2023seattle, coords = c('LONGITUD', 'LATITUDE'))

axSeattleMap <- st_set_crs(axSeattleMap, 4326)

#Plot it:

ggplot(axSeattleMap) + 
  geom_sf(aes(color = FATALS), size = 1)

```
